title: Short Pieces from MS. Cotton Galba E. IX
summary: CME00081 is the repository copy of the University of Michigan encoding of Joseph Hall's 'Short Pieces from MS. Cotton Galba E. IX,' printed in Englische Studien 21 (1895), pp. 201-209. Hall directly edited selected passages from British Library Cotton MS Galba E IX, ff. 48vb-49rb and 50vb-51vb, and collated Sir Peny against Joseph Ritson's 1833 and Thomas Wright's 1841 printings. Hall numbered three pieces, but DIMEV 1.0 separates his final unit into two poems; the selection therefore contains four medieval works. A complete 492-canvas scan of the entire 1895 journal volume, complete scans of the Ritson and Wright volumes, and the complete DIMEV 1.0 data release are cached locally. The Cotton manuscript has no exposed public digital object in its current British Library record, so the manifest records a whole-object Imaging Services order scope and maps the two discontiguous work ranges separately. One parallel Bodleian witness has a one-image Digital Bodleian object at fol. 162v; it is cached as a complete capture of that published selection but is partial manuscript coverage and does not overlap the relevant texts at ff. 165vb-166ra.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00081
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00081
entities.description: A public transcription of Hall's complete nine-page article, retaining three numbered DIV2 units, printed page breaks, manuscript folio and column milestones, notes, and italicized abbreviation expansions or supplied text.

entities.label: Joseph Hall, Short Pieces from MS. Cotton Galba E. IX (1895)
entities.description: Hall's edited selection of three numbered units from Cotton Galba E IX, containing four intellectually distinct Middle English poems. The edition prints manuscript folio and column references, italic expansions and supplied readings, glosses, emendations, and collations against Ritson and Wright for Sir Peny.

bibliographic.citation: Joseph Hall, 'Short Pieces from MS. Cotton Galba E. IX,' Englische Studien 21 (1895), 201-209.
bibliographic.title: Short Pieces from MS. Cotton Galba E. IX

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Galba E IX, ff. 3-114
alternate_labels: London, British Library, MS Cotton Galba E. ix
entities.description: A parchment composite codex of three originally independent manuscripts associated at an early stage. Hall's discontiguous selection lies near the end of Part I: the transitoriness poem at ff. 48vb-49rb, Sir Peny at ff. 50vb-51rb, and the two Passion poems at f. 51va-b; the Prophecies of Merlin intervenes at ff. 49rb-50vb.

identifiers.value: Cotton MS Galba E IX, ff 3-114

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.value: First quarter of the fifteenth century

holding.shelfmark: Cotton MS Galba E IX, ff 3-114

physical_description.extent: Main cataloged item ff. 3-114; a separately cataloged Psalter leaf is f. 2
physical_description.format: Composite codex in three early-associated parts: ff. 3-51, 52-75, and 76-114
notes: Part I contains the four selected poems amid other Middle English works; the selection is not a continuous manuscript anthology.
The Database of Middle English Romance records Northern origin for the witness. This is witness-production evidence, not a common composition place for the poems.
The British Library record requires a letter of introduction for physical consultation.

entities.label: Sir Peny in Ritson's Pieces of Ancient Popular Poetry, second edition (1833)
entities.description: The first known printing of the Cotton Sir Peny text. The prefatory note says Ritson had intended it for the collection; the posthumous second edition prints it from Cotton Galba E IX, then numbered fol. 47b, and notes a differing 45-line fragment in Caius MS 174. At line 13 it supplies 'ofte' from the Caius witness.

bibliographic.citation: Joseph Ritson, Pieces of Ancient Popular Poetry from Authentic Manuscripts and Old Printed Copies, 2nd ed. (London: William Pickering, 1833), 103-108.
bibliographic.title: Pieces of Ancient Popular Poetry from Authentic Manuscripts and Old Printed Copies

entities.label: Sir Penny in Wright's Latin Poems Commonly Attributed to Walter Mapes (1841)
entities.description: Thomas Wright's appendix printing of the Cotton text, headed 'English, of the Fourteenth Century.' Wright identifies Cotton Galba E IX, then numbered fol. 47v, mentions the differing Caius copy, and states that the Cotton ballad had already appeared in Ritson's second edition.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Wright, ed., The Latin Poems Commonly Attributed to Walter Mapes (London: Camden Society, 1841), 359-361.
bibliographic.title: The Latin Poems Commonly Attributed to Walter Mapes
bibliographic.publisher: J. B. Nichols and Son for the Camden Society

entities.label: Cambridge, Gonville and Caius College, MS 174/95
entities.description: A parallel, substantially differing 93-line witness of Sir Peny at pp. 484-486. It is not Hall's base text, but Ritson's edition uses its reading 'ofte' at Cotton line 13 and Hall reports that indirect supply.

notes: DIMEV records 93 lines and the manuscript title 'Explicit de dynario Johanne magistro.'

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Don. c. 13
entities.description: A fourteenth-century parchment codex of sermons and religious material. DIMEV indexes parallel versions of both Passion poems together at ff. 165vb-166ra: the appeal begins at 165vb and is followed by a five-stanza version of the second poem.

physical_description.extent: At least 168 leaves; relevant additions at ff. 165vb-166ra
notes: The Digital Bodleian object publishes only fol. 162v, so it does not show either related poem.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Rawl. poet. 175
entities.description: A parchment codex of the second half of the fourteenth century containing the Prick of Conscience and religious and moral poems. DIMEV indexes the first Passion poem at f. 80rb and the second at f. 93va, separated within the manuscript.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.value: Second half of the fourteenth century

notes: The repository catalog groups the relevant material under 'Religious and moral poems.'

entities.label: Bavarian State Library complete scan of Englische Studien 21 (1895)

The local bundle retains every provider canvas at a practical 1600-pixel-width derivative except native-size fallbacks explicitly recorded in its inventory.

access.access_method: No complete public digital object or digitised-content link was exposed in the current exact item record; use Imaging Services for a mediated whole-object reproduction request.

Submit the British Library online digitisation order form using the shelfmark. For a complete physical-source surrogate, ask whether f. 2 is currently part of the same bound object, and request f. 2 plus ff. 3-114 when physically applicable, front and back boards or covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every manuscript leaf recto and verso, edges when offered, and any inserted, detached, or separately housed matter the repository can reproduce. Request full color from the original where permitted. State separately that the work occupies ff. 48vb-49rb and 50vb-51vb.

access.format: Repository-supplied digital photographs or available surrogate scans
notes: British Library guidance says original manuscripts are photographed rather than scanned and asks requesters to specify recto, verso, and color requirements.
A request for only 'the work' would omit most of the manuscript and would not satisfy complete physical-source coverage.
Large, fragile, or difficult material may require individual discussion and pricing.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Discontiguous manuscript passages containing four poems
editorial_practices.description: Hall selects ff. 48vb-49rb and 50vb-51vb, omitting the intervening Prophecies of Merlin, and groups the final two poems under one Roman numeral and heading.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript abbreviations, omitted letters, and editorially supplied readings
editorial_practices.representation: Italic type within the Middle English text
editorial_practices.description: Hall's typography distinguishes many expanded or supplied letters in italics; notes explicitly identify manuscript omissions and additions, so italics must not be flattened into ordinary manuscript text without qualification.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Cotton Sir Peny and prior Ritson and Wright editions
editorial_practices.representation: Footnotes reporting variant readings and a Caius-supplied word
editorial_practices.description: Hall compares Sir Peny with Ritson and Wright and records their readings; at line 13 he retains italic 'ofte', absent from Cotton and supplied from the Caius manuscript by Ritson.
The U-M/CME encoding preserves Hall's italics with inline HI markup rather than reconstructing the manuscript abbreviation signs.
notes: The encoding records Hall's printed representation; only a manuscript facsimile can show the original abbreviation forms.

review_notes: The immediate source relationship was checked against the complete BSB journal scan rather than inferred from U-M metadata alone.
Hall's three numbered units and the four-work DIMEV boundary are preserved simultaneously instead of forcing one structure to erase the other.
Current Cotton foliation is used for the witness; Ritson's and Wright's old fol. 47b/v references are retained only as historical locators.

Complete-source caching is object-relative: the Hall cache contains all 492 journal-volume canvases, while the Don. c. 13 cache contains all one canvas of a provider-selected object but remains partial coverage of the manuscript.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00081.xml.
evidence.summary: Confirms repository identity, U-M identifiers, Hall title and signature, three DIV2 units, printed page breaks, manuscript milestones, notes, italic markup, and complete encoded text.

evidence.citation: Structured XPath audit of CME00081.xml.
evidence.locator: DIV2 1-3 and all L, LG, Q, PB, MILESTONE, and NOTE elements
evidence.summary: Finds 23,783 bytes; 275 L elements, 48 LG or Q groups, 9 PB elements, 68 MILESTONE elements, and 51 NOTE elements. DIV2 line counts are 94, 123, and 58; the last comprises four Latin Q/L lines and 54 Middle English lines.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, 'Short pieces from ms. Cotton Galba E.IX / [ed. Joseph Hall],' CME00081.
evidence.summary: Confirms the title, Hall credit, Leipzig publisher, 1895 date, complete article text, public access, and exact-item public-domain / No Copyright - United States statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection page.
evidence.summary: States that the collection's later batches were manually keyed from public-domain editions and proofread in-house, makes XML available for bulk download, and applies No Copyright - United States to collection materials.

evidence.citation: Joseph Hall, 'Short Pieces from MS. Cotton Galba E. IX,' Englische Studien 21 (1895), 201-209.
evidence.summary: Primary source for the exact selection, editor, article pages, Cotton loci, three numbered units, first-publication statement, Ritson and Wright references, manuscript readings, supplied text, glosses, and signature dated Manchester, 21 February 1895.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek, IIIF manifest for Englische Studien 21 (1895), bsb11665433.
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete 492-canvas object, title, Leipzig: Reisland 1895 publication, BSB shelfmark P.o.angl. 413 h-21, BSB-ID, URN, canvas labels and image requests, and NoC-NC rights statement. Bundle integrity, member hashes, first and last surfaces, and target canvases were audited.

The target image is 1600 x 2486 sRGB TrueColor; first and last surfaces use native-size fallbacks recorded in inventory.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Galba E IX, ff 3-114, record 041-001607569.
evidence.locator: Item identity, date, language, access, user conditions, custodial history, and Digitised Content field
evidence.summary: Confirms the exact current shelfmark and item boundary, Middle English and Latin script, 1400-1424 date range, Cotton Collection custody, Richard Chawfer and Robert Cotton provenance, letter-of-introduction requirement, London physical-access route, and absence of an exposed digitised-content link.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services and online ordering guidance.
evidence.locator: Online ordering; required information; manuscript, color, delivery, and permissions guidance
evidence.summary: Provides the current order-form route, required shelfmark/title/folio information, recto-verso conventions, reference-versus-reproduction image options, original-manuscript photography rule, color-request instruction, fee notice, and licensing contact.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026.
evidence.locator: Preservation of originals; turnaround; pricing; copyright and reproductions
evidence.summary: States single-page supply, possible alternative formats for fragile originals, individual handling of large orders, variable pricing, and that supplied images must not be copied or reproduced without written permission.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, London, British Library, MS Cotton Galba E. ix.
evidence.locator: Date, origin, material, dimensions, codicological parts, and contents ff. 48vb-51vb
evidence.summary: Supports the 1400-1425 date, Northern witness origin, parchment and dimensions, three originally independent parts, and exact sequence: transitoriness poem ff. 48vb-49rb; Prophecies of Merlin ff. 49rb-50vb; Sir Peny ff. 50vb-51rb; appeal poem f. 51va; second cross poem ff. 51va-51vb.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0 source-data release, records 339, 2489, 201, and 3387.
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml records 339, 2489, 201, 3387; data/Bibliography.xml Hall1895b, Ritson1791, Wright1841b
evidence.summary: Versioned scholarly evidence for four distinct poem identities, IMEV/NIMEV numbers 189, 1480, 110, and 2080, incipits, subjects, verse forms, Cotton loci, Hall pages, Caius Sir Peny, and the Don. c. 13 and Rawl. poet. 175 Passion-poem witnesses. The bundled README and LICENSE apply CC BY 4.0.
notes: Record 201's Cotton source note says 54 lines even though its incipit and explicit correspond to Hall's first 18 Middle English lines and record 3387 separately accounts for the next 36; the discrepancy is retained as an open question.

evidence.citation: Joseph Ritson, Pieces of Ancient Popular Poetry from Authentic Manuscripts and Old Printed Copies, 2nd ed. (London: William Pickering, 1833), 103-108.
evidence.locator: title page; Sir Peny prefatory note and pp. 103-108; p. 104 note to verse 13
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete 1833 second edition, identifies Cotton Galba E IX f. 47b under older foliation as the base, describes the Caius 174 fragment, states that the poem is 'now given,' and records 'ofte' from MS Caius at verse 13.

open_questions.question: Can the British Library supply a complete color surrogate of the entire current Cotton MS Galba E IX physical object, including f. 2 when physically bound with ff. 3-114 and all binding and endmatter surfaces?
next_steps: Submit the whole-object Imaging Services request scope recorded in access:bl-cotton-reproduction.

Ask the repository to clarify whether separately cataloged f. 2 is part of the same present physical object and whether covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, and detached matter can be photographed.
Record the quote, delivered coverage, resolution, color, terms, and exact work folio-to-image mapping when the repository responds.

open_questions.question: What written permission and fees would apply to sharing any British Library-supplied Cotton facsimile in the Git repository or using its images in commercial or noncommercial print-on-demand publications?
next_steps: Ask permissions@bl.uk and licensing@bl.uk for terms covering public Git redistribution, scholarly quotation, and commercial or noncommercial publication.
Keep supplied images in the ignored research cache unless written permission authorizes redistribution.
Separate permission to study the images from permission to publish or redistribute them.

open_questions.question: Did Hall work directly from Cotton Galba E IX throughout, or from an unrecorded transcript or photographs for any portion?
next_steps: Consult Hall's papers, correspondence, and fuller editorial statements if provenance at that granularity becomes necessary.
Retain the direct-transcription relation as strongly supported rather than documentarily proven.

open_questions.question: Does DIMEV record 201's '54 lines' note intentionally describe Hall's combined third unit rather than the 18-line poem delimited by that record's incipit and explicit?
next_steps: Ask DIMEV to clarify the record note.
Preserve the separate IMEV 110 and 2080 boundaries and Hall's combined print structure in parallel.

open_questions.question: Can complete public or requestable color facsimiles be obtained for Gonville and Caius MS 174/95, Bodleian MS. Don. c. 13, and Bodleian MS. Rawl. poet. 175?
next_steps: Request complete-codex imaging quotes from the holding libraries, naming the target pages or folios separately.
Do not treat Digital Bodleian's single Don. c. 13 image at fol. 162v as target-text or complete-codex coverage.
Cache any authorized public complete objects and record exact target canvas ranges.
